title: limacia selwynii
intro: Limacia selwynii, also known as the New Zealand tea tree or Kawakawa, is a native plant species found in New Zealand. It is a climbing shrub with heart-shaped leaves and has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves of the Limacia selwynii contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Limacia selwynii can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and creams, and are often used to promote relaxation, reduce pain, and improve overall well-being.
